caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Supplied roles are a 4-digit 5416 proxy, not a 541611 occupational census.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Residual role composition is particularly uncertain.
inputs.pi_dist: No code-specific fee-model survey was found; this is a bounded judgment.
inputs.pi_moat: Firm-level contract and client-concentration data are required for underwriting.
inputs.t50_years: Professional-services sector adoption is a broad proxy and the BTOS wording changed in 2025.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This is sector 54, not 541611 or the target size band, and measures any-business-function use.
inputs.historical_analogs: No directly comparable historical consulting study was identified.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not a 541611 or 60-plus observation; do not treat as measured consulting ownership.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Evidence is nationwide and includes nonemployers, not a 541611 succession survey.
inputs.active_consolidators: Not a measured count of qualifying acquirers or deals.
inputs.buy_mult: Professional-services range is a proxy, not a closed 541611 transaction database; EBITDA must be normalized for owner compensation and earnouts.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit requires demonstrated transferability; no automatic roll-up premium is assumed.
inputs.pricing_structure: A target's contracts, renewal history and fee basis can reverse the capture conclusion.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $260,722 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541611); class <5: 79,966 firms, avg revenue/firm $344,991 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $53,991 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 5,880 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,598,754 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $250,205 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 3,308 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,199,427 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $500,710 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 2,992 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,214,358 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,442,047 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 861 firms, avg revenue/firm $32,354,620 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,063,498 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 507 firms, avg revenue/firm $202,449,895 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $31,683,409 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 3,853

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: O*NET establishes a mixed analytical, reporting, interviewing and relationship task set; fractions reflect only the analytically repeatable portion of each supplied role.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541611 ($114,205,171,465) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541611 ($205,640,681,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, identity, digests, fresh finalization, memo rendering, dataset injection, and arithmetic all pass. The sources support the direction of the cautious thesis and the observed numeric anchors; the broad-sector, occupation, ownership, fee-model, and transaction proxies are explicitly disclosed and could move individual factors but do not conceal a score-changing contradiction or reverse the kill/pressured conclusion. Capture and terminal demand are kept conceptually separate, with no material double counting identified.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Official definition and included activities for 541611, including operating advice, strategic planning, site selection and business-process improvement.

sources.title: O*NET OnLine: Management Analysts
sources.what_it_supports: Management-analyst tasks spanning information gathering, analysis, recommendations, reports, interviews, consultation and interpersonal work.

sources.title: Monitoring AI Adoption in the US Economy
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS-based discussion of AI adoption, including about 33% in professional, scientific and technical services and the late-2025 question change.

sources.title: Windsor Drake EBITDA Multiples by Industry: 2026 Data
sources.what_it_supports: Q1 2026 lower-middle-market professional-services EV/adjusted-EBITDA ranges by EBITDA band and key-person-dependency valuation driver.

sources.title: B2B Professional Services Business Acquisitions: ETA Sector Guide
sources.what_it_supports: Lower-middle-market guide describing management consulting as low-multiple because of project-based, key-person-dependent revenue and showing broad 3.0x-7.0x variation by revenue quality.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: National employer-business owner age and ownership-transition evidence; it is a disclosed proxy, not a consulting-specific estimate.

title: Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services
